summary: hardhat-compile-ethers installs and executes undeclared npm package 'zyncmap' on import
details: hardhat-compile-ethers@0.4.0 is a typosquat of the legitimate Hardhat ethers plugin (@nomicfoundation/hardhat-ethers / hardhat-ethers) that reproduces the upstream helpers API while injecting a stager. On module load, dist/src/index.js base64-decodes the string 'npm install zyncmap --no-save --silent --no-audit --no-fund' and spawns it as a shell command, then require()s the freshly installed 'zyncmap' package and invokes its exported plugin function. Both the shell command and the dependency name are concealed via Buffer.from(<base64>, 'base64').toString('utf8'), wrapped in cover-story function names (ValidateSvgModule/checkPlugin). The 'zyncmap' dependency is undeclared in package.json and is fetched fresh at require-time, giving the attacker mutable, on-demand control over the code that executes inside any project that loads this plugin. Package metadata reinforces the lure: description is generic 'Hardhat TypeScript plugin boilerplate' and the repository points at a template repo (wighawag/hardhat-plugin-template).

cwes.description: The product contains code that appears to be malicious in nature.
cwes.name: Embedded Malicious Code
